title: App design from project requirements
use_when: App design from project requirements. Use when the user needs a product workflow for design & prototyping related to app design from project requirements. Trigger terms: app-design, ux, design-system, wireframing.
do_not_use_when: Do not use App design from project requirements when the request is mainly metric diagnosis, pricing, GTM, or implementation planning without UX evidence; route to the narrower Productize skill that owns that artifact instead. Do not use it when the user lacks enough context to distinguish facts, assumptions, and decision risk.
output_artifact: App design from project requirements UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
framework_fit: App design from project requirements fits Design work in the Design lifecycle when the user needs App design from project requirements UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks and has enough product context to make tradeoffs explicit. Use it to produce a decision-ready artifact, not a framework tour.
failure_modes: Produces App design from project requirements UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks without tying recommendations to the user's Design stage, evidence, and decision pressure.
Treats App design from project requirements as generic Design advice instead of naming assumptions, missing inputs, risks, and the next validation step.
Fails to route away from App design from project requirements when another Productize skill owns the requested artifact more directly.

examples.prompt: Use $app-design-from-project-requirements to turn this app context into a decision-ready App design from project requirements UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks.
examples.expected_artifact: App design from project requirements UX/design review with findings, constraints, fixes, and acceptance checks
